title: Cooperative Law and Procedure: An Appraisal of the Nigerian   Educational System
author: Akanji, Ajibola Anthony
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The basic argument in this paper is that as currently constituted, legal education in Nigeria  is neither holistic nor inclusive. In this instance Nigerian legal education as offered in  universities and the Nigerian laws school does not accommodate cooperatives. The  assertion is premised on the preliminary findings from review of relevant literature on  legal education in Nigeria, and the Nigerian cooperative movement. Amongst other  findings, the circumstance supports the sub-optimal utilisation of cooperative resources  and hinders the bottom-up mobilisation of collective resources for the implementation of  sustainable development. The foregoing brings to fore the unintended contribution of  deficient legal education to underdevelopment. Recommendations were proffered to  remedial the identified deficiencies.
